heading: Office of the Ombudsman for Analytic Objectivity
label: 3525 Office of the Ombudsman for Analytic Objectivity
notes: Change of Name, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. W, title IV, § 401(b)134 Stat. 2377Dec. 27, 2020“Any reference in any law, regulation, map, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the Ombudsman for Analytic and Collection Objectivity of the Central Intelligence Agency shall be deemed to be a reference to the Office of the Ombudsman for Analytic Objectivity of the Central Intelligence Agency established by section 24(a) [] of the Central Intelligence Agency Act of 1949 ( et seq.), as added by subsection (a).”50 U.S.C. 3525(a)50 U.S.C. 3501

W, title IV, § 401(b)134 Stat. 2377Dec. 27, 2020“Any reference in any law, regulation, map, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the Ombudsman for Analytic and Collection Objectivity of the Central Intelligence Agency shall be deemed to be a reference to the Office of the Ombudsman for Analytic Objectivity of the Central Intelligence Agency established by section 24(a) [] of the Central Intelligence Agency Act of 1949 ( et seq.), as added by subsection (a).”50 U.S.C. 3525(a)50 U.S.C. 3501

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established in the Agency an Office of the Ombudsman for Analytic Objectivity (in this section referred to as the “Office”).

children.heading: Appointment of Ombudsman
children.label: 2 Appointment of Ombudsman
children.text: The Office shall be headed by an Ombudsman, who shall be appointed by the Director from among current or former senior staff officers of the Agency.
on an annual basis, conduct a survey of analytic objectivity among officers and employees of the Agency;

implement a procedure by which any officer or employee of the Agency may submit to the Office a complaint alleging politicization, bias, lack of objectivity, or other issues relating to a failure of tradecraft in analysis conducted by the Agency;

children.label: A
children.text: summarizes the facts relevant to the complaint;

children.label: B
children.text: documents the determination of the Ombudsman with respect to the complaint; and

children.label: C
children.text: contains a recommendation for remedial action;

if a complaint submitted pursuant to paragraph (2) alleges politicization, bias, or lack of objectivity in the collection of intelligence information, refer the complaint to the official responsible for supervising collection operations of the Agency; and

continuously monitor changes in areas of analysis that the Ombudsman determines involve a heightened risk of politicization, bias, or lack of objectivity, to ensure that any change in the analytic line arises from proper application of analytic tradecraft and not as a result of politicization, bias, or lack of objectivity.

On an annual basis, the Ombudsman shall submit to the intelligence committees a report on the results of the survey conducted pursuant to subsection (b)(1) with respect to the most recent fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of complaints of submitted pursuant to subsection (b)(2) during the most recent fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the nature of such complaints, the actions taken by the Office or any other relevant element or component of the Agency with respect to such complaints, and the resolution of such complaints.

children.label: A
children.text: a list of the areas of analysis monitored during the most recent calendar quarter pursuant to subsection (b)(5); and

children.label: B
children.text: a brief description of the methods by which the Office has conducted such monitoring.

children.heading: Intelligence committees defined
children.label: d Intelligence committees defined
children.text: In this section, the term “intelligence committees” means the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives and the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate.
